trails.name: Progress Water Race Track
trails.description: Cross the Inangahua suspension bridge to follow this track up stairs and along the old Progress Water Race.

trails.name: Cedar Flat Hut to Toaroha Rd End Track
Lake Kaniere Walkway
trails.description: Walk or mountain bike along the western shores of Lake Kaniere through lush lakeside forest, passing many picturesque bays and numerous forest streams.

trails.name: Old Coach Road
trails.description: This pleasant forest walk starts and finishes near Greyneys Shelter, 6 km east of Arthur’s Pass Village.

trails.name: Tailrace Walk
trails.description: This short Nelson Creek walk crosses a number of impressive historic tailraces.

trails.name: Carew Falls Track
trails.description: The track winds up through forest to reach a waterfall on Carew Creek. Enjoy the view from sitting on immense water-shaped granite boulders.

trails.name: Kaniere WRWW - Lake to Ward Road
Paparoa Tk - Lookout to Lwr Pororari Bri
Kaniere WRWW-Wards Rd to Kendy Crk
Taramakau Valley to Townsend Hut Route
Hokitika Gorge Walk - Platform to Rocks
Camp Creek Route
trails.description: A backcountry route to Camp Creek Hut with the option of following a ridge to the summit of Mount Alexander.

trails.name: Arahura Track to Newton Creek Hut
Harper Pass Track
trails.description: This track runs from Hurunui swing bridge to Harper Pass. It is popular for day walks or overnight stays in three huts. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Temple Basin Track
trails.description: A nature photographer’s dream, experience expansive mountain views and alpine birdlife on this track to Temple Basin.

trails.name: Otira Valley Track
trails.description: Follow a deep alpine valley under the northern flank of Mount Rolleston/Kaimatau to the Otira River footbridge.

trails.name: Morrison Bridge to Pfeifer Ck FloodTrack
Hawdon Hut Track
trails.description: A good introduction to backcountry tramping with multiple river crossings. This is a suitable trip for families if the river is not too high.

trails.name: Woolshed Hill Track
trails.description: A steep climb on Woolshed Hill, with excellent views over the Waimakariri and Hawdon Valleys.

trails.name: Casey Hut to Poulter Hut Track
Fossil Creek to Bullock Creek Road Track
Fox River Cave Junctn to Fossil Ck Pt Tk
Cons Track
trails.description: This steep, rugged track leads to the bushline on Mt Cassidy. It is suitable for experienced trampers only.

trails.name: Edwards Hut to Hawdon Hut (via Tarn Col)
Carrington Hut to Waimakariri Fall Hut
Carringto Hut to Browning Pass Route
Bullock Creek to Pororari River Track
Colls Dam  Walk
trails.description: This Nelson Creek track leads to a pleasant outlook above the historic Colls Dam.

trails.name: Velenski Walk
trails.description: The walk climbs to a ridge of unmodified forest with fine examples of trees including rimu, miro and kahikatea.

trails.name: Waiuta Town Walk
trails.description: Explore Waiuta, one of the West Coast’s best known ghost towns.

trails.name: Dobson Nature Walk
trails.description: Enjoy interpretation points along this walk and seats with good views of the surrounding mountains.

trails.name: Croesus Track Barrytown to Paparoa Track
Waiuta to Big River Track
trails.description: Walk or mountain bike an old benched pack track past old mining sites to Big River Hut.

trails.name: Mt Bovis Route
trails.description: An expert route leading to panoramic views of the Paparoas and out to the Tasman Sea. Suitable for fit, experienced and well-equipped people.

trails.name: Larrys Creek Track
trails.description: Follow Awarau or Larry River to get to a historic gold mine.

trails.name: Capleston to Kirwans Hut Track CLSD
Paparoa Tk- Smokeho Carpk-Ces Clark Hut
Fox River Caves Track  - END PART CLSD
Alborns Walk
trails.description: Walk through old mine workings on this well-graded loop track.

trails.name: Styx Roadend to Grassy Flat Hut Track
Hunt Saddle to Hunts Creek Hut Track
Kelly Carpark to Hunt Saddle Track
Carroll Hut to 7 Mile Creek Bushline Trk
Browning Pass to Harman Hut Track
Harman Hut to Mudflats Hut Track
Lwr Arahura Hut to Arahura Rd End Track
Mudflats Hut to Lower Arahura Hut Track
Lathrop Saddle to Styx Valley Route
Ballroom Overhang Track
trails.description: Experience some of the Paparoa National Park's finest limestone features, including a large limestone outcrop.

trails.name: Grassy Flat Hut to Sty Saddle Track
Kelly Carpark to Carroll Hut Track
7mile Creek Bushline to Dillons Hut Trk
Harmon Pass to Julia Hut Track
Julia Hut to Mid Taipo Hut Track
Mid Taipo Hut to Taipo Rd End Track
Top Kokatahi to Crawford Junc Hut Route
Zit Saddle to Top Kokatahi Hut Route
Paparoa Tk - Lower Pororari River Track
Paparoa Tk - IPT Junction to Waikori Rd
Murray Creek Track
trails.description: Walk or mountain bike this loop walk through beech forest and historic goldfields.

trails.name: CrawfordJunc Hut to Lathrop Saddle Route
Top Crooked Hut to Tops Route
Newton Creek Hut to Newton Saddle
RoadEnd to Jacko Flat Hut Route
Jacko Flat to Top Crooked Route
Dunns Creek Route to Newton Saddle
Waikiti Route
trails.description: A backcountry route that follows the Waikiti River.

trails.name: Kaniere Wards Rd to Hurunui Jacks TRAIL
Loopline Rd to Kawhaka Pass TRAIL
Paparoa Tk-Ces Clark Hut-Moonlight T Hut
Paparoa Tk- Moonlight T Hut-Pororari Hut
Paparoa Tk- Pororari Hut-Inland Pack Tk
Arthur’s Pass Walking Track
trails.description: Experience the best of Arthur’s Pass's diverse alpine vegetation, waterfalls, wetlands, rich history and stunning mountain views on this walk.

trails.name: Avalanche Peak - Crow River Route
trails.description: This route makes a good weekend trip, all against a backdrop of some of the best features of Arthur’s Pass National Park – glaciers, mountain peaks and ice-fed rivers. Crow Hut is at the bush edge in an alpine meadow.

trails.name: Avalanche Peak Route
trails.description: Avalanche Peak is the only peak in Arthur's Pass that is marked by a poled route to the summit. The climb is 1,100 m vertical from the village and over just 2.5 km horizontal. On a fine day, you will be rewarded with grand views of the surrounding peaks.

trails.name: Lake Brunner (Moana) (south) walks
trails.description: Walk through wetlands around the shore of Lake Brunner, or to an impressive waterfall.

trails.name: Big River tracks
trails.description: Explore a remarkable array of goldmining relics in Big River, one of the most intact mining areas.

trails.name: Brunner Mine Site Walk
trails.description: Explore the fascinating and family-friendly historic Brunner Mine and enjoy the interactive displays.

trails.name: Coal Creek Track
trails.description: Enjoy the lovely bush walk down to the impressive Coal Creek Falls. A nice place to cool off with a swim in the hot summer months.

trails.name: Croesus Track
trails.description: Croesus Track is one of the finest surviving examples of a miners' track on the West Coast. Croesus Track is part of Paparoa Track and a premier mountain bike ride.

trails.name: Hokitika Gorge Walk
trails.description: This loop track showcases the stunning Hokitika Gorge and the pristine mature podocarp forest of the Hokitika Scenic Reserve.

trails.name: Kaniere Water Race Walkway
trails.description:
The Kaniere Water Race Walkway provides an opportunity for
a pleasant bush walk in an area that is rich in history.

trails.name: Kirwans Track and Waitahu Valley
trails.description: The walk up to Kirwans Hut is one of New Zealand's classic overnight tramps.  A three day loop can also be made by going down the Waitahu Valley.  There are incredible historic relics to be seen in this area.

trails.name: Lake Kaniere short walks
trails.description: These short forest walks lead to picnic areas, waterfalls and sandy beaches ideal for swimming - plenty to keep kids interested.

trails.name: Lake Kaurapataka Route
trails.description: This track starts and finishes at Aickens car park beside SH73, 5 km west of Ōtira township.

trails.name: Mingha - Deception Route
trails.description: This route is a classic traverse of the Southern Alps, crossing over Goat Pass. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Moonlight Pack Track
trails.description: The Moonlight Pack Track offers the opportunity to see reminders of the gold mining history of the area, and to walk through West Coast native beech forest.

trails.name: Mount Tuhua Track
trails.description: This is a steep tramp to the open tussock peak of Mt. Tuhua (1125 m) and is suitable for fit people only.

trails.name: Nelson Creek walks
trails.description: Nelson Creek is an excellent spot to go walking, camping and exploring. With historic water races, tunnels, recreational gold fossicking and a number of short walks, there is plenty to do.

trails.name: Punakaiki Pancake Rocks and Blowholes Walk
trails.description: Explore a limestone landscape of pancake-shaped rock formations, blowholes and surge pools on this short, paved walk that's great for children.

trails.name: Point Elizabeth Walkway
trails.description: A stunning, well surfaced, coastal walkway. Enjoy walking through varied and interesting forest to a panoramic, cliff top viewing platform. Point Elizabeth track is a perfect walk for families and children.

trails.name: Woods Creek Track
trails.description: This fascinating forest track passes by tailraces and dams and through tunnels built by gold miners in the 19th century.

trails.name: Inland Pack Track
trails.description: Experience some of the finest features of Paparoa National Park.

trails.name: Carroll Hut Track
trails.description: Climb steeply up 760 m to Carroll Hut for good views (on fine days) down the Taramakau River to the West Coast.

trails.name: Truman Track
trails.description: Walk through unspoiled subtropical forest to a spectacular coastline.

trails.name: Cave Creek Memorial Track/Kotihotiho
trails.description: Walk down to the deep, narrow chasm of Cave Creek and be surrounded by moss-covered rocks and overhanging trees.

trails.name: Pororari River tracks
trails.description: Follow a spectacular limestone gorge on a short walk, or make a loop for a longer walk. The Pororari River Track is the pedestrian entry/exit for the Paparoa Great Walk.

trails.name: Goldsborough short walks
trails.description: Explore the area around Goldsborough Campsite on these short walks. Kids will love the option with tunnels.

trails.name: Goldsborough (Shamrock) Track
trails.description: Walk through an historic gold mining area with mining tunnels, dams, sluice faces, tailings and water-races.

trails.name: Mount Te Kinga Track/Ara O Te Kinga
trails.description: Climb to a lookout for spectacular views, or continue to the summit of Mount Te Kinga.

trails.name: Mount French Track
trails.description: Climb to the top of Mount French for spectacular views of Lake Brunner and the Southern Alps.

trails.name: Lake Brunner (Moana) (north) walks
trails.description: Explore the area around Moana township and Arnold River, and enjoy lake and river views.

trails.name: Paparoa Track: Pororari Hut Track
trails.description: Walk to Pororari Hut for 360 degree views and experience a shorter (overnight) version of the Paparoa Track Great Walk.

trails.name: Elizabeth Hut Route
trails.description: A backcountry route that follows the Haupiri River to Elizabeth Hut, with further travel possible over the Trent Saddle.

trails.name: Crooked Route
trails.description: Trampers on this route up the Crooked River valley should have backcountry navigation skills.

trails.name: Brian O’Lyn Route
trails.description: A backcountry route that takes you above the bush-line and on to Lake Morgan hut.

trails.name: Devils Punchbowl Walking Track
trails.description: Walk through lush beech forest to a stunning 131 m waterfall where you can feel the full force and sound of the fresh mountain waterfall.

trails.name: Dolomite Point Walk
trails.description: Wheelchair/Pram Track

trails.name: Kahikatea Walk   Lake Kaniere
trails.description: Wheelchair/Pram Track

trails.name: Kings Walk Domain
trails.description: A zigzag uphill track to four lookouts. Five minute walk to the first lookout provides a view of the town centre and Grey River. Carry on to the second lookout 10 minutes further on to view central Greymouth. Be careful from here on to the third lookout as the track is not well formed but is considered easily negotiated for walkers. Spectacular lookout across the lower Grey Valley areas.

trails.name: Kowhai Bush Walk
trails.description: A loop walk of approximately 700 metres consisting of a narrow zone of Kowhai Forrest, half of which is bounded by the mudflats of Blaketown Lagoon. Some parts consists of boardwalks which follow through estuarine vegetation and unsuspecting wildlife.

trails.name: Mt Brown Hut   Geologist Creek Route
trails.description:
Mt. Brown Hut is a community initiative that was undertaken by Eddie Newman and Julia Bradshaw. The Hut began its life as Lower Arahura Hut in the valley of the same name, and spent its first 50 years there. It was a standard NZFS 4-bunk S81 design, built in 1962. When DOC decided to build a new Hut at the site in 2010, Eddie and Julia were able to convince them to fly the dismantled sections of the old hut out to be reassembled on Mt. Brown. The project ended up becoming a total rebuild due to the plethora of building code and safety requirements that didn't exist in the 1960's when the Hut was built. Considerable community input, volunteer labour and donations made it possible for the project to reach fruition. The Permolat Group contributed financially and with volunteer input and the Hut was opened for public use in November 2010. It was an instant success, debuting in Wilderness Magazine, outdoor calendars, DOC posters, and the cover of "Shelter From The Storm," a book on high-country huts by Rob Brown, Geoff Spearpoint and Shaun Barnett. The Hut is now profiled on local tourist brochures and i-Sites and the majority of visitors are now foreign tourists or travellers. The high levels of visitation show no sign of abating. In 2017 Eddie and Julia emigrated to Christchurch and delegated oversight of the Hut to the Permolat Group. Hugh van Noorden from the Permolat Trust has kindly taken on the lead role for the the project.

Mt. Brown Hut has no fixed fee system and is reliant on a koha (donation) for its upkeep. Koha means a contribution, not just donate-if-you-feel-like-it. The Hut has a small firebox and because there is no good wood supply at 1100m, coal needs to be flown in. Supplies tend to get used up very quickly and when it runs out it is not uncommon for less conscientious types to start hacking into the living alpine scrub around the Hut. Contributions also go towards hut maintenance and upkeep of the access tracks. Sadly, the majority of visitors are not donating. This Hut is not DOC funded so don't be freeloader. Donations are payable to Permolat Trust, Kiwibank, 38-9016-0266330-00. Our Charities Registration No. is CC50626. Please specify that the donation is for Mt. Brown so we can earmark it for that purpose.

trails.name: Omotomotu Bush Walk
trails.description: A 25 minutes loop walk within the capabilities of most walkers, through the only substantial forest area close to Greymouth. Halfway through the walk is a five minute deviation to a lookout point with views of regenerating forest.

trails.name: Reefton Walkway
trails.description: Enjoy this 2.7-km loop trail near Reefton, West Coast. Generally considered an easy route, it takes an average of 46 min to complete. This trail is great for hiking and walking, and it's unlikely you'll encounter many other people while exploring
